title: MD
biography: Avicenna (Ibn Sina) was a Persian polymath whose 'Canon of Medicine' served as the standard medical textbook in Europe and the Islamic world for over 500 years. He described contagion, introduced quarantine, and systematized clinical pharmacology.
aiSummary: Avicenna is a medicine specialist at Various courts of Persian rulers. Based in Iran.

timeline.title: Completed 'The Canon of Medicine'
timeline.description: Compiled the most comprehensive medical encyclopedia of the medieval world, used as a textbook for 500+ years
